title: Names
wikitext:
[]: Names             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Oct 16, 2010 9:54 am Quick question from someone whos taken in a lot about Eva but doesnt know a great deal about Japan and Japanese culture. I know about the significance of some of the surnames and middle names but would the first names and surnames generally be seen as unusual or common or even funny in Japan? Guess what im asking is, for example, whether Makoto Hyuga, Maya Ibuki and Shigeru Aoba is like having three characters in a western programme called Dave Jones, John Smith and Sue Jenkins? :P

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 16, 2010 10:11 am It's a little more difficult to answer than it would be in a Western show, because Japanese names tend to cycle in and out of popularity much more rapidly than English-language names. That said: Makoto, to use your example, is a pretty common name - more so than, say, Shigeru, or Maya. As for the rest of the cast - there are plenty of Asukas out there, a lot of Shinjis, a decent number of Reis and Kaworus...probably not too many Gendous or Kouzous...Hikari, Touji, Kensuke are all reasonably popular names. The surnames would be seen as unusual if you met a person in real life with that name - I mean, you wouldn't expect to meet too many people named Soryu in real life, it's the name of a sunken ship, not a person. But in the context of a fictional work, people are mostly willing to roll with it.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Oct 16, 2010 10:12 am A lot of Evangelion characters were named after Japanese battleships.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Oct 16, 2010 10:17 am it's the name of a sunken ship, not a person. But in the context of a fictional work, people are mostly willing to roll with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 16, 2010 10:33 am Jayfive wrote: True enough, not sure if a UK or US show would get away with calling a character Charlie Titantic or Mary Lusitania :P

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Sat Oct 16, 2010 1:21 pm There is a bit here on the wiki where Anno goes into how he came up with all the names: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Character_Name_Origins

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Oct 16, 2010 4:46 pm For what I know, 'Makinami' is made up/take´n from a battleship, and so is 'Illustrious'. It could be her Middle Name, tough. There is that trend of people in english speaking countries (more the US than GB, tough) giving their kids the most impossible Names... But what about 'Mari'? Is that an actual common Japanese Name?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 16, 2010 5:32 pm Kendrix wrote: But what about 'Mari'? Is that an actual common Japanese Name?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Oct 17, 2010 8:52 am Mr. Tines wrote: Well, Ms Illustrious isn't the only anime girl I've seen called Mari .

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Oct 17, 2010 10:59 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: But Mari is an "original" Japanese name or it's been imported from Mary/Marie and co.?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Oct 17, 2010 12:34 pm Ornette wrote: a quick google search reveals: http://www.issendai.com/rpgs/japanese-girls-names-4.shtml http://www.babynamescountry.com/origins/japanese_girl_baby_names5.html http://www.20000-names.com/female_japanese_names.htm#MARI http://www.babynames.org.uk/japanese-girl-baby-names.htm http://www.japan-guide.com/forum/quereadisplay.html?0+56339 on the first page. Looks common enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Oct 17, 2010 4:14 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I wasn't implying that it wasn't common, I was just asking for the origins of the name, thank you (and next time I'll try to make a better search before asking, there's also a wikipedia entry for this name). I was a little puzzled because Makinami is usually written in kanji, Illustrious in katakana and Mari in katakana as well and I was considering her European origins as well. Anyway it's not uncommon to see Japanese names written in katakana (for my own joy) in Evangelion so I was simply wondering about it.

[]:             Quote Post by jojojoker » Mon Oct 18, 2010 1:25 am Kendrix wrote: I was asking because since she is british, it could also be 'Mary', but the one time she says her name, (on the phone, just after poor Shinji almost suffocated under her) she pronounces it with a long 'a' and not with the 'eh' you'd find in 'Mary'
